stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 40 factory workers. The sample mean is 23.0 sec with a sample standard deviation of 6.4 sec. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 23.0 sec. 95% CI = [21.0, 25.0]. Point estimate: 23.0 sec.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
